symptoms.symptom_name: Severe migratory polyarthralgia (wrists, MCPs, both knees)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Articular flare of systemic lupus erythematosus
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Migratory, non-erosive inflammatory polyarthralgia is the commonest lupus articular pattern.
Viral arthritides also cause symmetric migratory polyarthralgia.
Septic arthritis is usually monoarticular and fixed, not migratory across many joints.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Articular/constitutional flare of systemic lupus erythematosus
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Constitutional fatigue is a hallmark of lupus flare activity.

symptoms.symptom_name: Known lupus with non-erosive joint disease (risk factor / context)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Articular flare of systemic lupus erythematosus
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A pre-existing lupus diagnosis with prior articular flares makes a recurrent flare the prior-probability leader.

symptoms.symptom_name: Hydroxychloroquine non-adherence over the past week (precipitant)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Articular/constitutional flare of systemic lupus erythematosus
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Lapses in hydroxychloroquine reliably precipitate lupus flares given its long half-life; identifies a reversible driver.
Absence of pleuritic pain lowers the probability of serositis but does not exclude it without auscultation.
Normal breathing reduces the likelihood of significant pericardial/pleural or pulmonary involvement.
Absence of visible haematuria/dark urine is reassuring but nephritis can be clinically silent — only urinalysis and bloods can assess it.

symptoms.symptom_name: No high fever or rigors (home thermometer 37.2)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of high fever/rigors lowers the probability of joint or systemic sepsis, though afebrile septic arthritis occurs.

symptoms.symptom_name: No single hot, red, tensely swollen joint

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Septic arthritis classically produces a single exquisitely hot, red, tensely effused joint; its absence argues against but does not exclude infection.

symptoms.symptom_name: No new malar rash or oral ulcers

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Articular/constitutional flare of systemic lupus erythematosus
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mucocutaneous activity can accompany a flare; its absence neither confirms nor excludes an articular flare.

symptoms.symptom_name: Joints tender to palpation and painful on active range of motion, without tense effusion, marked heat or intense erythema

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Articular flare of systemic lupus erythematosus
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Inflammatory but non-septic joint signs support a flare — a clinician-only examination finding, sealed.
Absence of a tense, hot, erythematous effusion argues against septic arthritis — but requires in-person palpation to establish.

symptoms.symptom_name: No pericardial friction rub; heart sounds normal (auscultation)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absent friction rub argues strongly against pericarditis — a clinician auscultation finding the AI Doctor cannot obtain, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clear lung fields, no pleural rub (auscultation)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clear auscultation argues against pleuritis — clinician-only finding, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician-measured vitals: BP 120/75, HR 88, RR 16, SpO2 100%, Temp 37.2

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Articular/constitutional flare of systemic lupus erythematosus
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal haemodynamics and no fever support a non-critical flare — clinician-measured in the source, sealed; the patient-reported temperature (37.2) is admissible in 01 but the remaining vitals are not.

symptoms.symptom_name: Urinalysis / urine protein-creatinine ratio and inflammatory bloods (FBC, CRP/ESR, creatinine, complement, anti-dsDNA)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Only urinalysis, protein quantification and bloods can assess renal involvement and flare activity — unavailable in telehealth, hence prompt in-person review.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Migratory non-septic polyarthralgia + fatigue + missed DMARD in known lupus
symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Articular/constitutional flare of systemic lupus erythematosus
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Together these four make a precipitated articular flare the leading diagnosis with an identified reversible driver.
This negative cluster is what a telehealth clinician CAN gather to lower the probability of the dangerous mimics — but none of these mimics can be fully excluded without in-person exam and tests.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person clinical examination (joint palpation, cardiac and chest auscultation)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The definitive step: examine the joints for a septic focus and auscultate for a rub — the very findings that are inaccessible via chat and that gate exclusion of serositis and septic arthritis.
Screens for lupus nephritis, which can be clinically silent; abnormal results change urgency substantially.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Inflammatory bloods and lupus serology (FBC, CRP/ESR, creatinine, C3/C4 complement, anti-dsDNA)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Quantifies flare activity, screens for cytopenias and renal impairment, and helps distinguish flare from infection.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Joint aspiration and synovial fluid analysis
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Only indicated if examination reveals a single hot, tensely swollen joint suggesting septic arthritis; not warranted for a migratory non-hot polyarthralgia, and never performed via telehealth. The AI Doctor should not over-order this remotely.
